1	-- schema.sql — applied by sqlite.Open on every call (idempotent via
2	-- CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS). Kept as a single file per the pitch's
3	-- no-migration-library stance: if the schema ever changes before v1.0,
4	-- users re-run `sense scan` and the index rebuilds from source.
5	
6	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_files (
7	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
8	    path        TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,
9	    language    TEXT    NOT NULL,
10	    hash        TEXT    NOT NULL,
11	    symbols     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
12	    indexed_at  TEXT    NOT NULL
13	);
14	
15	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_files_path     ON sense_files (path);
16	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_files_language ON sense_files (language);
17	
18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_symbols (
19	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
20	    file_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
21	    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
22	    qualified   TEXT    NOT NULL,
23	    kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,
24	    visibility  TEXT    DEFAULT 'public',
25	    parent_id   INTEGER REFERENCES sense_symbols(id),
26	    line_start  INTEGER NOT NULL,
27	    line_end    INTEGER NOT NULL,
28	    docstring   TEXT,
29	    complexity  INTEGER,
30	    snippet     TEXT,
31	    name_parts  TEXT,
32	    -- (file_id, qualified) is the Index contract's natural key for Symbol
33	    -- upserts. Not in the definition doc but required for ON CONFLICT.
34	    UNIQUE (file_id, qualified)
35	);
36	
37	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_name      ON sense_symbols (name);
38	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_qualified ON sense_symbols (qualified);
39	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_kind      ON sense_symbols (kind);
40	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_file_id   ON sense_symbols (file_id);
41	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_symbols_parent_id ON sense_symbols (parent_id);
42	
43	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_edges (
44	    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
45	    source_id   INTEGER REFERENCES sense_symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
46	    target_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
47	    kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,
48	    file_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sense_files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    line        INTEGER,
50	    confidence  REAL    DEFAULT 1.0
51	);
52	
53	CREATE INDEX        IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_source ON sense_edges (source_id, kind);
54	
55	-- idx_sense_edges_target is the reverse-edge index the blast BFS
56	-- reads against: "given these target_ids, find source_ids for
57	-- kind='calls'." Including source_id makes the index COVERING for
58	-- that exact query shape so BFS walks with zero row fetches per
59	-- hop — `EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN` reports `USING COVERING INDEX` and
60	-- `internal/sqlite/plan_test.go` pins that guarantee. The DROP
61	-- below is an in-place upgrade path for databases that were created
62	-- before the index gained its third column; on a fresh DB it is a
63	-- no-op.
64	DROP   INDEX        IF EXISTS     idx_sense_edges_target;
65	CREATE INDEX        IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_target ON sense_edges (target_id, kind, source_id);
66	
67	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sense_edges_unique ON sense_edges (source_id, target_id, kind, file_id);
68	
69	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_metrics (
70	    key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71	    value INTEGER NOT NULL
72	);
73	
74	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sense_meta (

74	type Adapter struct {
75		db *sql.DB
76		// Rebuilt is true when Open detected a schema version mismatch and
77		// dropped all tables to recreate a fresh schema. Callers that need
78		// a populated index (e.g. the MCP server) should trigger a full
79		// scan before serving.
80		Rebuilt bool
81		// FTSMigrated is true when Open detected a stale FTS5 table
82		// (missing columns) and dropped+recreated it. Keyword search
83		// results will be incomplete until the next scan repopulates them.
84		FTSMigrated bool

90	// Open opens or creates a SQLite index at path. The parent directory must
91	// already exist. On every open the schema is reapplied (idempotent) and
92	// WAL + foreign-key pragmas are set via the DSN so they apply to every
93	// connection in the pool, not just the first.
94	func Open(ctx context.Context, path string) (*Adapter, error) {
95		// modernc.org/sqlite reads _pragma parameters on every new connection,
96		// which is what foreign_keys (a per-connection setting) requires.
97		// synchronous=NORMAL is durable under WAL for crash recovery; it can
98		// lose the last few seconds under a power cut. That's the right
99		// trade-off for a derived index — `sense scan` rebuilds from source.
100		mmapSize := "0"
101		if strconv.IntSize == 64 {
102			mmapSize = "134217728" // 128MB on 64-bit platforms
103		}
104		dsn := "file:" + path + "?_pragma=foreign_keys(1)" +
105			"&_pragma=journal_mode(wal)" +
106			"&_pragma=synchronous(normal)" +
107			"&_pragma=temp_store(memory)" +
108			"&_pragma=cache_size(-8000)" +
109			"&_pragma=mmap_size(" + mmapSize + ")"
110	
111		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sqlite open: %w", err)
114		}
115	
116		// Single connection serializes writes and reads. Scan is sequential
117		// today; relax when concurrent MCP reads warrant the contention cost.
118		// Load-bearing for InTx — see maxOpenConns constant.
119		db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxOpenConns)
